canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, ImageNet-1k is predominantly characterized as a large-scale benchmark dataset used to measure `Image classification`. Most definitions describe it as containing over a million images organized into 1,000 object categories, and its most frequent application is for evaluating core classification accuracy, though it is also widely used for pre-training vision models. A smaller but notable line of work employs ImageNet-1k to evaluate `Image generation`, with one paper calling it a "gold standard benchmark" for this task and another specifying the use of FID and Inception Score for assessing quality. Beyond these uses, the dataset is also operationalized to measure a range of other behaviors, including `Zero-shot image classification`, `Multimodal alignment`, and `Object recognition`. The data shows varied applications, from using its validation set to construct typographic attacks to modifying its images into flattened patches to evaluate cross-modal compression. Some studies also use it to assess `Generalization` and `Multimodal understanding`. The provided data also defines related but distinct benchmarks, such as `ImageNet-A`, which is specifically used to evaluate model robustness against natural adversarial examples.

definitions.text: Standard benchmark for image generation and classification, used here to evaluate class-conditional image generation quality using FID and Inception Score.

definitions.text: A large-scale dataset for object recognition and image classification containing over 1.2 million images across 1,000 categories.

definitions.text: An out-of-distribution benchmark for ImageNet containing natural adversarial examples that are challenging for standard models.
